steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim to illustrate: the soldiers’ collective mood becomes strongly affected by the strenuous conditions of the journey. The correct quotation must show (1) physical hardship and (2) a resulting emotional or psychological effect on the group.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘"The sierra is clad in gala colors. Over its inaccessible peaks the opalescent fog settles like a snowy veil on the forehead of a bride."’: Describes the landscape’s appearance — no soldiers, no mood, no hardship. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘"All day long [the soldiers] rode through the canyon, up and down the steep, round hills, dirty and bald as a man's head, hill after hill in endless succession."’: Describes the terrain and pace but shows no effect on the soldiers’ mood or mental state. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘"Then, hurriedly, [the soldiers] took the Juchipila canyon northward, without halting to rest until nightfall."’: Mentions movement and direction — no hardship causing a mood shift. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘"The sun, beating down upon [the soldiers], dulled their minds and bodies and presently they were silent."’: The sun (strenuous condition) directly causes a collective mental and behavioral effect (dulled minds, silence). This is the only choice that shows the causal link the claim requires. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
